name: prpm-json-best-practices
description: Best practices for structuring prpm.json package manifests with required fields, tags, organization, multi-package management, enhanced file format, eager/lazy activation, and conversion hints
capabilities: Guide creation of prpm.json manifests for single and multi-package repositories
Explain required fields, format types, and subtype values for all package types
Provide organization patterns for tags, file paths, and version management
Detail eager vs lazy activation, enhanced file format, and conversion hints
Validate manifest structure and detect common mistakes like duplicate names
Show collection creation for bundling existing packages together
author: AgentWorkforce
securitySummary: This skill is purely documentation and educational content about PRPM package manifest structure. All 519 static findings are false positives from pattern matching against documentation examples, JSON schema definitions, and bash command examples. No executable code or actual security risks present.
valueStatement: Creating proper PRPM package manifests is complex with many required fields and best practices. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on structuring prpm.json files for single packages, multi-package repositories, collections, and advanced features.
limitations: Documentation only - does not automatically generate or validate prpm.json files
Requires understanding of package management concepts and semver versioning
Does not cover PRPM CLI usage or publishing workflows in detail
Focuses on manifest structure, not package content quality

useCases.title: Publish first PRPM package
useCases.description: Learn required fields, file structure, and validation steps to create a valid prpm.json and publish your first package

useCases.title: Manage multi-package repositories
useCases.description: Organize multiple related packages in one repository with proper naming, tagging, and version synchronization

useCases.title: Create package collections
useCases.description: Bundle existing published packages into curated collections with version ranges and dependency management

promptTemplates.title: Create basic manifest
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a prpm.json manifest for a Claude skill called typescript-safety that enforces TypeScript best practices. Include all required fields.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-package setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I have three separate prpm.json files for different packages. Show me how to combine them into one multi-package manifest with proper organization.

promptTemplates.title: Collection creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a collection that bundles typescript-strict version 1.0.0, react-best-practices version 2.0.0, and testing-utils latest into a development-setup collection.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: My package targets both Cursor and Claude. Add conversion hints so it uses alwaysApply for Cursor and allows Read/Write tools for Claude.

bestPractices: Always use full file paths from project root starting with .claude/ or .cursor/ directories
Validate manifest with duplicate name checks and file existence verification before publishing
Keep related packages at the same version and organize by privacy, format, then subtype
antiPatterns: Do not add installed packages from prpm.lock to prpm.json - they serve different purposes
Avoid using prepublish scripts instead of prepublishOnly as they run on user install too
Never use absolute paths or missing directory prefixes in files array

faq.question: Do I need prpm.json if I just install packages?
faq.answer: No, prpm.json is only needed if you are publishing packages. Regular users only need prpm.lock which is auto-generated.

faq.question: What is the difference between packages and collections?
faq.answer: Packages define files to include from your repository. Collections bundle existing published packages by packageId references.

faq.question: Can I have both packages and collections in one prpm.json?
faq.answer: Yes, use the packages array to define your packages and collections array to bundle them or reference external packages.

faq.question: When should I use eager loading for skills?
faq.answer: Use eager: true only for critical skills that must always be active like coding standards. Most skills should be lazy loaded on demand.

faq.question: Why do my file paths need .claude or .cursor prefixes?
faq.answer: File paths must be full paths from project root where prpm.json lives. This is needed for tarball creation and installation.

faq.question: What are conversion hints used for?
faq.answer: Conversion hints provide format-specific metadata when converting packages between different AI tool formats like Cursor to Claude.
